message:
test(rename): runtime_compat → runtime_async in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/ (ft-y378j.1)

Mechanical sed rename across 79 test files / 615 occurrences. Scope:
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/*.rs only — production source rename
(crates/frankenterm-core/src/*.rs and crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs)
is the sibling bead ft-y378j.2.

Symbols affected (all preserved via the deprecated
`pub use crate::runtime_async as runtime_compat` alias in
frankenterm-core/src/lib.rs, so this is a pure name-update — no
behavioural change):

  use frankenterm_core::runtime_compat::*  →  use frankenterm_core::runtime_async::*
  use crate::runtime_compat::*             →  use crate::runtime_async::*
  crate::runtime_compat::sleep_with_cx     →  crate::runtime_async::sleep_with_cx
  crate::runtime_compat::timeout_with_cx   →  crate::runtime_async::timeout_with_cx
  ... etc.

Build verification (CC=llvm-clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc6-target,
fork-bypass for the rch hook):
- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --tests: zero new errors from the
  rename. The 4 remaining `unresolved import` errors all reference
  `frankenterm_core::replay_*` modules (replay_counterfactual,
  replay_fault_injection, replay_guardrails, replay_scenario_matrix)
  that moved to frankenterm-core-replay under cc7's lane — pre-existing
  baseline, not introduced by this rename.

Net diff: 79 files / +615 / -615 — pure renaming. No behavioral change.
The deprecated `runtime_compat` alias remains active in lib.rs; tests
that the alias preserves continue to compile against the new name
because runtime_async IS the underlying module.

Closes ft-y378j.1 (P2, child of ft-y378j parent reality-check finding).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -31,8 +31,8 @@ fn run_async_test<F>(future: F)
 where
     F: std::future::Future<Output = ()>,
 {
-    use frankenterm_core::runtime_compat::CompatRuntime;
-    let runtime = frankenterm_core::runtime_compat::RuntimeBuilder::current_thread()

         .enable_all()
         .build()
         .expect("failed to build test runtime");
